newsSince: No new disclosure since June 9; the +15.8% move is largely specialty-foundry/sector flow. The relevant fundamentals remain May news: VSMC Singapore JV capacity trimmed to 44k wpm (from 55k) due to added interposer/complexity but first phase sold out under LTAs, with the fab seen fully loaded earlier than 2029, plus TSMC interposer outsourcing and GaN licensing.

sources.label: Taipei Times - Vanguard adjusting new fab plans

verdicts.rankRationale: Manufacturing-tollgate lens unchanged since June 9: same VSMC 12-inch Singapore JV (first output 2027), TSMC interposer outsourcing, GaN licensing. A +15.8% no-news move is flow, not node repositioning. Hold.
verdicts.bull: Interposer outsourcing from TSMC + GaN make VIS a specialty AI-supply tollgate; VSMC sold-out phase 1.
verdicts.neutral: Specialty foundry, not advanced-node; capacity trimmed; upside priced after +102% YTD.
verdicts.bear: Mature-node pricing/geopolitics; VSMC ramp is 2027+; flow-driven rerate.

verdicts.rankRationale: On the CPO lens VIS's TSMC interposer outsourcing is the most direct alpha vector; the May VSMC sold-out/added-interposer commentary keeps the advanced-packaging tollgate intact and modestly de-risks the 'single-bundle validation' flag. Small up vs CPO peers; no new disclosure since June 9.
verdicts.bull: Interposer capacity is a packaging bottleneck for CPO/HBM; sold-out LTAs validate demand pull.
verdicts.neutral: Indirect CPO play; benefits flow through TSMC, not VIS-branded optics.
verdicts.bear: Interposer is commodity-ish; capacity cut signals execution complexity; +102% YTD.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Interposer capacity ramp for advanced packaging
